entry.title: The behavior of "DATA()=0" for booked and unbooked data
entry.content:
Introduction

DATA() = 0  sets only booked cells to zero and does not affect unbooked cells. 

On the other hand, DATA() = 0 + 0 (Calculation result) sets all cells within the calculation scope to zero.

Example

Dimension

  • Date
  • Entity
  • Product

Calculation scope

  • Date = "202410", "202411", "202412"
  • Entity = "US"
  • Product = "Product_01", "Product_02", "Product_03"

Case 1: Booked Data

  • Calculation scope: Booked data

         

 

  • Advanced Formulas script

 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = ("202410", "202411", "202412")
MEMBERSET [d/Entity] = "US"
MEMBERSET [d/Product] = ("Product_01", "Product_02", "Product_03")

DATA() = 0

 

  • Calculated Result: The value of the booked cell has been changed to zero.

          

 

Case 2: Unbooked Data

  • Calculation scope: Unbooked data

         

 

  • Advanced Formulas script

 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = ("202410", "202411", "202412")
MEMBERSET [d/Entity] = "US"
MEMBERSET [d/Product] = ("Product_01", "Product_02", "Product_03")

DATA() = 0

 

  • Calculated Result: The unbooked cell is not changed to zero.

          

 

Case 3: Booked + Unbooked Data

  • Calculation scope: Booked + Unbooked data

         

 

  • Advanced Formulas script

 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = ("202410", "202411", "202412")
MEMBERSET [d/Entity] = "US"
MEMBERSET [d/Product] = ("Product_01", "Product_02", "Product_03")

DATA() = 0

 

  • Calculated Result: The value of the booked cell has been changed to zero, and the unbooked cell is not changed to zero.

          

        

Case 4 : Booked + Unbooked Data

DATA() = 0+0 (or 0-0)

  • Calculation scope: Booked + Unbooked data

         

 

  • Advanced Formulas script

 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = ("202410", "202411", "202412")
MEMBERSET [d/Entity] = "US"
MEMBERSET [d/Product] = ("Product_01", "Product_02", "Product_03")

DATA() = 0 + 0

 

  • Calculated Result: The value of the booked and unbooked cell has been changed to zero.

          

       Moonjun

entry.title: Re: SAP HCM for SAP Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Hello,

In a system conversion project, the whole system will be converted from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA. This means it is not possible to select only specific modules.

If you only need to move HCM, the option is a carve-out project. However, for that, you need to involve the DMLT team (Data Management and Landscape Transformation). I would suggest discussing it with your SAP Account Executive.

Regards,

Jose M.

entry.title: can I use CORRESPONDING # for deep internal tables?
entry.content:
as of now I am doing the value assignment using FOR LOOP as shown below, I was curious the same can be done using MAPPING keyword?

Source and Target structure are not identical. Source is a simple internal table and target is a deep internal table.

 

et_ds_rel_map_upd = VALUE #( FOR <fs_ds_rel_map> IN lt_ds_relmap_upd_U
                             ( %key-ds     = <fs_ds_rel_map>-ds_id
                               %key-dsfld  = <fs_ds_rel_map>-ds_fld
                               %key-rcon   = 'M'
                               sourceds    = <fs_ds_rel_map>-for_ds_id
                               sourcedsfld = <fs_ds_rel_map>-for_ds_fld
                          %control = VALUE #( sourceds = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                           sourcedsfld = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                          sourcedsexpr = if_abap_behv=>mk-on )
                             ) ).

 



Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Delete more than one line at once on a sales order
entry.content:
You can speed the process up by clicking in any field on teh row to delete and press CTRL + K.
This will instantly remove the line. The line beneath will automatically be selected, so pressing CTRL + K multiple times will remove as many subsequent lines as pressed.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Question: In your opinion, what's the biggest use-case for Blockchain at SAP Custo
entry.content:
this is a really good question, 

Enterprise Distributed (Blockchain) Ledgers, as a product, out of the box, are many times more secure than traditional databases

Enterprise Blockchain is more secure because of four characteristics

. Immutable

. Hash Mechanism

. Distributed

. Concensus

This makes the Enterprise Blockchain both a Secure Store of Data, and, a Secure Data Communication Channel

Considering these characteristics & capabilities, Enterprise Blockchain can be positioned in Enterprise IT Architecture as a Technology Standard 

Following Enterprise Architecture Demand Processes, the use cases will then find the Enterprise Blockchain, the use cases which are many are suited to situations including,

Common Source of Truth for Data within an Organisation or between Organisations

Out of the box, the most secure store of Data in the Organisation

This is SAP's explanation:

https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/what-is-blockchain.html

these are some of my explanations:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/why-i-love-sap-and-blockchain-databases-and-why-you-should-too/ba-p/13625869

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-enterprise-architecture-positioning-blockchain-database-as-an/ba-p/13629842

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-enterprise-architecture-let-the-use-case-find-the-blockchain/ba-p/13632458

Here is a publicly available Enterprise Blockchain running on the SAP BTP Kyma Service https://atkrypto-blockchain-for-sap.ad8ba34.kyma.ondemand.com/

entry.title: Why Do Enterprises Need ERP Software for Successful Digital Transformation?
entry.content:
In recent years, Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software has been one of the highly talked about topics. Once seen as just a tool for backstage operations, ERP software has evolved into a stepping-stone in the digital transformation journey of enterprises. Wondering how? Let’s break it down!  

Digital transformation is all about leveraging technology to improve your business, especially how your customers interact with you. ERP software helps you do this by integrating and managing all aspects of your business, like how you keep track of what you have, how you get things made during transformation, etc.  

In a way, ERP software acts as a foundation for your digital transformation initiatives. It has the caliber to make your transformed operations more efficient, profitable, and competitive. 

Reports have shown that the ERP software market is expected to reach up to USD 238.79 billion by 2032, highlighting its importance across industries. 

Therefore, we have decided to delve deeper into this topic. Today, we will discover why ERP matters more than ever for new-age enterprises, how it can facilitate successful digital transformation, and much more. So, let's get begin! 

Why Is ERP More Than Just a Software for Next-Gen Enterprises? 

Not just one, there are several reasons that companies are highly prioritizing ERP software now. We have covered a few of the core reasons by focusing on its role in supporting digital transformation initiatives. Let's dig in: 

  1. The Need for Enhanced Collaboration 

Have you ever found yourself chasing down data from various departments, only to realize that no one is on the same page? Frustrating, right! That's where ERP software facilitates better collaboration across diverse departments by providing a unified platform for communication and data sharing. It eradicates confusion and ensures all information flows seamlessly across departments such as, sales team knows what inventory is available, finance understands cash flow in real-time, and customer service can anticipate client needs based on data-driven insights. In turn, you’ll not just get streamlined operations but drive tangible value. 

  1. Empowering Agility in the Face of Disruption 

In today's market, if you're not adaptable, you're already falling behind. That's where ERP software gives you the ability to pivot quickly. Whether it's responding to a sudden market demand or adapting to customer feedback, having a robust ERP system means you can make decisions backed by real-time data. No matter whether your competitor launched a new product overnight or a sudden event shifted customer behavior. With ERP, you wouldn't just be reacting; you'd be ready to counter with your own innovations, equipped with the insights needed to stay ahead of the curve. 

  1. Driving Customer-Centric Innovation 

Remember, modern-day customers are not just looking for a transaction; they want a personalized experience that feels tailored to their actual needs. An effective ERP system empowers you to deliver just that. By smartly leveraging data analytics, you can get actionable insights into customer preferences and behavior that enables you to anticipate their needs before they even arise. Imagine being able to offer bespoke solutions that not only meet but exceed customer expectations. That's the kind of innovation that sets you apart from the competition. 

How ERP Software Can Revolutionize Digital Transformation 

As digitalization squeezes every industry, finding effective ERP software is imperative to adapt to shifting tides, predict storms, and give your business a competitive edge. Here's how it can revolutionize your operations and set the stage for future growth: 

  1. Automation: The Foundation of Digital Transformation 

Automation is one of the prime ways by which ERP software contributes to enterprise digital transformation. When businesses get overwhelmed by the evolving demands of the digital age, ERP helps them by automating business processes and streamlining operations with efficiency. From basic invoicing to inventory management, it automates processes at every stage of transformation. Moreover, it reduces manual work, preserves data accuracy, and lowers operational cost to keep your team focused on strategic initiatives that drive real revenue. 

  1. Future Readiness: Preparing for What's Next 

Next-gen ERP software isn't just streamlining your existing operations, it also strengthens your back for future readiness in a 2x faster and more dynamic digital age. Whether it's AI, IoT, or any other trend, ERP is designed to integrate seamlessly with emerging technologies. It is a platform that grows with you, not one that becomes obsolete in a few years. This flexibility is all we need to navigate through the next-gen digital world. 

  1. Process Optimization: High on Impact with Less Friction 

Let's be very honest—every organization has inefficiencies that can slow us down. In that case, custom ERP solutions can help you analyze your workflows, eliminate bottlenecks, and optimize your operations as required. It aims to not just save time but maximize your resources to create value. When you optimize your processes, you can deliver better products and services efficiently. This results in faster time-to-market, happier customers, and ultimately, a healthier bottom line. 

  1. Enhanced Analytics and AI: Insights that Drive Action 

Data is magical but only if you know how to refine and utilize it. ERP with advanced analytics and AI capabilities helps you turn raw data into actionable insights. It enables you to forecast trends and make data-driven decisions that truly move the needle. 

It would be just like having a built-in business intelligence team whispering strategic advice in your ear to stand out in the dynamic digital age. 

  1. Cloud ERP: Flexibility and Accessibility for the Modern Workforce 

Finally, let's not overlook the power of cloud ERP. In a world where remote/hybrid work is becoming the norm, having a cloud-based solution is essential. It provides your team the flexibility to access critical information anytime, anywhere. This means your team can work remotely, collaborate in real time, and stay productive regardless of location. Moreover, with cloud capabilities, you can easily scale your resources to meet changing demands without any burden of complex IT infrastructure. 

Are you Ready for ERP-Powered Digital Change? 

Indeed, we can't overlook the role of ERP in the new age of digital transformation. With each passing day, the digital world advances at a high speed and increasing the pressure to modernize and compete for enterprises. That's when ERP software provides the infrastructure required to streamline operations, enhance customer experience, and drive sustainable growth. 

Therefore, ERP is nothing short of our strategic partner in the digital transformation journey. In fact, 95% of organizations improved their business processes after implementing an ERP system. 

Trust me, with the expanding functionalities of ERP systems, it is worth considering for successful digital transformation initiatives.  

So, let's embrace the best ERP system and lead your organization into a future where digital change isn't just a goal but a reality. Let's make it happen!

entry.title: Personas 3.0 SAP GUI and Scripting - Does not work after an update on the laptop - Why?
entry.content:
Hi. 
I know the Java scripting is developed in SAP GUI Html and then it should use SAP Scripting to run the java scripts in SAP  GUI Windows. And I am the one developing the flavors by the way so I know how it works but I do not know the technical connection between SAP Personas java scripting developed in SAP GUI Html to the execution of the java in SAP GUI Windows.

It has worked for me all the way until now. An update happened to my laptop and since then the java scripting is not executed in SAP GUI Windows. With other words. SAP Personas does not work for me.
When I am logging into a citrix server (Same userID) and run the SAP from there then it works.
I have also tried on the client to remove the check marks for Scripting but actually even though I am doing this on my client in Citrix then SAP Personas still works and run the java but when I am doing it directly from my laptop where I am connected to the network through VPN it does not work. Nether with scripts activated or deactivated.
And until the update on my laptop I didn't have any issues at all. I have spent hours with the tech team and we cannot really find the issue.
Can anyone tell me which DLL, Setting or whatever is missing that might causing SAP Personas java scripts not to run on SAP GUI Windows.
Thanks
Thor

SAP Screen Personas

entry.title: BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT for populating Z fields in VBAK table - My expiriance
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

This short post should give a little help to developers which are tasked to fill custom Z fields in appended structure of VBAK table by processing inbound IDOC via FM IDOC_INPUT_COD_SALESORDER_CREA. My task was fairly simple: Write dates from inbound IDOC segment E1BPSDHD1 fields CT_VALID_F and CT_VALID_T into the custom Z fields of table VBAK, ZZPOCETAK and ZZKRAJ. This is step by step my suggestion to complete that task.

Step 1.  Append structures with these fields to structures: 

  • BAPE_VBAK – fields

     

  • BAPE_VBAKX - flags

     

  • VBAKKOZ

 

  • VBAKKOZX

  •  

Step 2. SE18 - Implement BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT, create a class with suggested name ZCL_IM_IDOC_DATA_INSERT with method from interface IF_EX_IDOC_DATA_INSERT~FILL.

Step 3. Fun part 🙂 Insert two segments E1BPPAREX, with information that needs to be saved in Z fields. Pay special respect to the actual place of your information in IDOC document. In my case I found that segment offset E1BPSDHD1 is not the same as in specification in WE60. Also find exact position of flags and fields for structures BAPE_VBAK and BAPE_VBAKX in E1BPPAREX segment. This segment should fill extensionin table in FM IDOC_INPUT_COD_SALESORDER_CREA. Do not increment segment number, segment number should be max number in your IDOC DATA table.

  method if_ex_idoc_data_insert~fill.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Purpose: Add custom segments to the IDoc data based on existing segments.
* Author: [Miljan Djuric]
* Date: [20241118]
* Following code is only for creating new documents. If you want to update existing, you will need VBELN field
*------------------------------------------------------------

data: ls_new_entry       type idoc_insert,
      ls_idoc_data       type edid4,
      lv_max_segnum      type edi_segnum,
      lv_parent_segnum   type edi_segnum,
      lv_hlevel          type edi_hlevel,
      lv_valid_from      type char08,
      lv_valid_to        type char08,
      lv_bape_vbak       type char60,
      lt_new_entries     type tab_idocins,
      lv_have_to_change  type c length 1.

  field-symbols: <fs_idoc_data> type edid4.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Find the highest segment number in the existing IDoc data
*------------------------------------------------------------
  lv_max_segnum = '000000'.

  loop at data assigning <fs_idoc_data>.
    if <fs_idoc_data>-segnum > lv_max_segnum.
      lv_max_segnum = <fs_idoc_data>-segnum.
    endif.
  endloop.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Read the parent segment 'E1BPSDHD1' to get its 'SEGNUM' and 'HLEVEL'
*------------------------------------------------------------
  read table data into ls_idoc_data with key segnam = 'E1BPSDHD1'.

  if sy-subrc = 0.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Extract 'Valid From' and 'Valid To' dates from positions in 'SDATA'
*------------------------------------------------------------
    lv_valid_from = ls_idoc_data-sdata+286(8).  " Start position 286, length 8
    lv_valid_to   = ls_idoc_data-sdata+294(8).  " End   position 294, length 8

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Prepare the first new segment 'E1BPPAREX'
*------------------------------------------------------------
    lv_bape_vbak = |{ 'BAPE_VBAK' width = 60 }|.  " Pad 'BAPE_VBAK' to 60 characters

    concatenate lv_bape_vbak lv_valid_from lv_valid_to into ls_new_entry-sdata respecting blanks.

  " Segment number for the new segment is max number in data table
    ls_new_entry-segnum  = lv_max_segnum.
    ls_new_entry-segnam  = 'E1BPPAREX'.
    ls_new_entry-counter = '000'.

  " Append the new segment to 'lt_new_entries'
    append ls_new_entry to lt_new_entries.

    clear ls_new_entry.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Prepare the second new segment 'E1BPPAREX'
* Flag structure is 30 in lenght and then you add number of filds with no blanks
* In this case 30 + 10 + 3 and then two XX for flags struc. BAPE_VBAKX
*------------------------------------------------------------
    ls_new_entry-sdata = 'BAPE_VBAKX                                 XX'.

  " Same segment number for the second new segment
    ls_new_entry-segnum  = lv_max_segnum.
    ls_new_entry-segnam  = 'E1BPPAREX'.
    ls_new_entry-counter = '000'.

  " Append the second new segment to 'lt_new_entries'
    append ls_new_entry to lt_new_entries.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Update protocol data
*------------------------------------------------------------
    protocol-stamid = 'ABVC'.
    protocol-stamno = '123'.
    protocol-repid  = sy-repid.

* Indicate that changes have been made
    have_to_change = 'X'.

*------------------------------------------------------------
* Insert new entries after the parent segment in the IDoc data
*------------------------------------------------------------
    if sy-subrc = 0.
* If parent segment not found, append to the end
      append lines of lt_new_entries to new_entries.
    endif.

  else.
* Parent segment 'E1BPSDHD1' not found, handle accordingly
  " [Optional] Log an error or take alternative action
  endif.

  endmethod.

 I hope this will save some time 🙂

entry.title: Re: sales billing quantity based on schedule lines
entry.content:
Hello @LydiaBoulkroune 

In VOV7, set the Billing Relevance to "B" (Relevant for Order-Related Billing - Status According to Order Quantity) for the relevant item category. Then, configure the copy control settings for the item category using the VTFA transaction code.

Please review and let me know if you have any questions

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Cloud Foundry app stops on its own by provisioned_user_cloudfoundry-runtime...@uaa.cf.us10-001
entry.content:
Hello

Our Cloud Foundry app on a trial account  is being stopped every night at around 1:15 by users provisioned_user_cloudfoundry-runtime-environment-admin1731417007@uaa.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

Every night the id of the users is changing: 

admin1731417007

admin1731771779

admin1731687785...

 

The events screenshot from the Cloud Foundry app is attached.

They are not the users entitled to manage our apps in BTP.

Could you please give a hint on the possible reasons of stopping the application?

 

And what are these users?

entry.title: Can anyone explain oss note 3427359 about ADS generating encrypted PDFs?
entry.content:
Hi Community

I shortly had the problem described in oss note 3427359. I couldn't  merge PDF files generated with ADS. I use class cl_rspo_pdf_merge for merging.

If i debugged the method CHECK_DOCUMENT  in class CL_RSPO_PDF_MERGE, the Find encrypt command would return sy-subrc = 0 and thus give the error message "Encryption not supported"

The strange thing is that the problem disappeared by it self - or I don't know what fixed it. I didn't change the programming.

The history is that first the merge worked, then I got the error and then suddenly after a few days it works again.

I have discussed this with basis department and the only thing I think may be relevant is that there was a problem with a certificate on the SAP server preventing  the usage of https.

Oss note 3427359 is not very clear and the solution suggestion seems to be nonesens, so I hope someone in the community can help me understand the OSS note, understand what happened and learn how I may prevent this error in future.

Regards Thomas

entry.title: SAP fiori application 2mb file size issue
entry.content:
Subject: Issue with Handling Large Files in Fiori Application File Upload

Hi all,

I’m currently developing a Fiori application that involves file upload and download functionalities. During the file upload process, the document or file is converted into a Base64 string and sent to the SAP backend service.

However, I’m facing an issue: the Base64 string is being stored as a STRING type in the backend. While a STRING in SAP can handle up to 999,999 characters, the Base64 string of a 2 MB file exceeds this limit, often reaching around 2.1 million characters.

This limitation is causing errors or incomplete data storage in the backend.

Has anyone encountered a similar challenge or have suggestions to resolve this issue?
I’d appreciate any ideas or best practices for handling large file uploads effectively, including alternative approaches to storing and processing these files in the backend.

Thank you in advance for your insights!

entry.title: Re: CBTA date format issue: Why is the &today& token and...
entry.content:
Hello,


CBTA token %today% is referring to system dates format. It corresponds to the currently applied MS Window settings regarding the date format. Change your system date format to get another format that match the test user settings in the System Under Test (other way around may be also possible).

- Or - if your test user is using any SAP System date format which cannot match any available Windows system date format, then you may use a custom VBS function of your choice that can be added by a developer into the RTL.

Best Regards,
Rémi

entry.title: Usage-based subscription with minimum fixed consumption per billing cycle
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have troubles achieving the following scenario in Subscription Billing and I would really appreciate your help.

Our customer sells usage-based subscriptions with multiple rate elements.
Pricing is managed with pricing schemes.
Pricing of usage-based rate elements is mainly linear or based on scales.
Usage-based subscriptions are invoiced based on consumption but a fix amount / minimum consumption is always invoiced.

Example:
Rate element R-USAGE has a unit price of 5 € / EA, with minimum consumption of 50 EA (monthly cycle) -> fixed monthly amount billed = 250€.
1. At the end of the month, consumption is 30 EA -> billed qty = 50EA / 250€.
2. At the end of the month, consumption is 60 EA -> billed qty = 60 EA / 300 €.

As a workaround, I used a recurring charge for the fixed qty/amount + a usage-based rate element with tiered pricing (1EA to {fixed qty} = 0€) but this would mean duplicating the number of rate elements involved.
Considering that in some scenarios we have up until 4 usage-based rate elements, my workaround would result in 8 rate elements which would mean also 8 lines on the invoice for only one subscription.
Also pricing maintenance would be more complicated.

Is there a more efficient way to set up described scenario?

Thank you in advance for the help.

Regards,
Elena

entry.title: Re: How can I configure Communication arrangement setup f...
entry.content:
Custom themes from SAP S/4HANA​

If the tenant has maintained a custom theme in SAP S/4HANA, the custom theme is passed to the S/Cube app runtime via the sap-theme query parameter. The theme will then be loaded from the SAP S/4HANA system and applied to the UI5 app. To load the theme from the SAP S/4HANA system, the URL of the SAP S/4HANA system of the tenant needs to be maintained in the XSUAA trusted domains configuration of the tenant. DwC will add those trusted domains of the tenant to the relevant Content Security Policies and to the allowed theme origins of the app runtime, so that the theme can be loaded from the SAP S/4HANA domain. Please note that it is not needed to integrate with the theme designer as the custom themes are loaded from the SAP S/4HANA system.

 

SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service Configuration Procedure

https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/eed6f62fbe144d7f87d7397d673a2ffe/58e579460fd940a3ba9b3b26084ff170.html

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

As follow-up to our previous release 2408, this blog post provides an update concerning our most recent release 2411, which is available for customers and partners since November 16th. This particular release features highlights such as ABAP Cloud in SAP Build, a new approach to build Application Jobs, and the region-less subscription of the Landscape Portal.

The complete list of all new features can be found on the What´s New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment page.

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Creating an ABAP Cloud Project in the Context of the SAP BTP ABAP environment, a service key is no longer needed when creating an ABAP Cloud Project. You now enter the ABAP Service instance URL (details)
  • Triggering Where-Used List for Usages of CDS Annotation to enable the investigation in which ABAP CDS an annotation is used
  • Displaying Field Characteristics for RAP Types in the Element Information Popup, The Element Information popup (F2) for RAP types shows additional field characteristics information such as mandatory:create, mandatory:execute or readonly (details)
  • Multi-Dimensional Analysis App Generator, you can now use the multi-dimensional analysis app generator wizard to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a multi-dimensional analysis Fiori app (details)
  • Support of SAP Fiori launchpad app descriptor items in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these items describe how an application is started and consist of a target mapping and one or more tiles, for more information see SAP Fiori Applications in the ABAP Environment and Working with Launchpad App Descriptor Items
  • Rerunning Unit Tests with Coverage in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • Clearing Coverage results, you can now clear your coverage results in the ABAP Coverage view (details)

ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC)

  • ATC Check "Usage of Released APIs" can be run for ABAP Cloud Objects

Reuse Services

  • New Class Interface for Application Job Catalog Entries and Templates, with the new attribute-based class interface it is now possible to change the parameters, sections, and groups of a catalog entry (details)
  • Business Rule Framework (BRF plus) is enhanced with a code generation service and an API introduced to facilitate the consumption of business rules within ABAP Cloud (details)
  • XCO XSLX: You can now style the cells of an Excel worksheet. You can also modify existing documents (details)
  • XCO CDS: You can now generate CDS external entities and logical external schemas, and you can now add comments and arithmetic expressions to CDS views (details)

UI Services and SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Creating Launchpad App Descriptor Items (details)

Integration

  • With Business Event Logging, you can capture and log business events locally within the ABAP environment
  • App Display Business Event Logs. It provides an overview of logged business events for different objects and event types. You can explore the details of the event with this app
  • App Display Business Events by Objects: It groups business events by instances of objects and object components
  • App Display Changes to Objects: You can see the changes for your objects on a field level as recorded in business event logging

Security

  • ABAP environment tenants are now equipped with a signed default client certificate, which is automatically rotated and can be used for customer managed integration scenarios
  • The communication scenario SAP_COM_0915 provides access to APIs to read the Read Access Logs from the system, the API can for instance be leveraged by a Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) system
  • TLS 1.3, we now support Transport Layer Security 1.3 for outbound HTTP, WebSocket RFC, and SMTP protocols
  • The system exposes APIs to retrieve customer-managed security settings in a read-only manner

Custom Code Migration App

  • Include ADT links in SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check Export, the export of ATC results for the SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check now includes HTTP(S) links, which enables you to navigate to the source code of example code in ADT for Eclipse. This new functionality is available in the Custom Code Migration app only (details)
  • New option to start analysis after project creation, you can now choose whether you would like to start the custom code analysis run automatically at project creation or whether you would like to schedule it manually after project creation (details)
  • Changes in Scope Calculation Automation, you now need to initiate scope calculation manually after project creation, the scope calculation no longer starts automatically at project creation (details)

Cloud ATC

  • Change Validity Date for ATC Exemptions in the Approve ATC Exemptions App (details)
  • Replication of ATC Exemptions from on-premise towards SAP BTP, Customers need to use transaction SATC_EXPORT_XMPT to download the exemptions from an on-premise ATC system (details)

Technical Monitoring

  • Enhanced app HANA Table Analysis: With the new Column Loads and Column Unloads screens in the HANA Table Analysis app, you can check how many columns of the tables in your HANA database have been loaded to or unloaded from the main storage (details)
  • New app HANA Memory Analysis: With the new app, you can get an overview of the memory consumption of the HANA memory components over time. This helps you to identify bottlenecks in your HANA memory (details)

Lifecycle Management

  • When cloning a software component, you can now optionally use the newly added parameters to request cloning a Bring Your Own Git (BYOG) repository (details)

abapGit

  • Support of object deletions, it can now be exported from and imported to the ABAP environment with abapGit (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Extended Access, you can now access and subscribe to the Landscape Portal for SAP BTP ABAP Environment from every region that is available for the ABAP environment. In regions without SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, some applications in the Landscape Portal are limited or not available (details)
  • Deinstalling Products, the new Undeploy Product app in the Landscape Portal enables you to permanently deinstall existing products (details)

entry.title: SuccessFactors Time Off: Converting time into a Savings account (France)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Today @LSOKPA and I bring you a custom solution for a very common requirement in France – Converting time into a Savings account (untaken leave days can be saved for employee retirement).

Each year the company launches a campaign that requests employees to select the number of days they would like to move to a specific time account (CET) and/or to the retirement plan.

The request is sent to HR Administrators who view and validate the number of days selected.

The approved days should be deducted from the annual leave bucket and moved to the CET account.

Procedure:

To allow employees to make the request we created a custom MDF Portlet where the below info is displayed.

Employees are able to select the number of days they would like to move to the CET account and/or to the retirement plan:

The remaining balance is a view only field and it is populated by the following business rule:

The MDF custom portlet can have a workflow associated to require approval from the HR Administrator. For further information related to the custom portlet please refer to the SAP documention – Implementing the Metadata Framework.

In order to automate the deduction from the annual leave bucket and the posting on the CET account we built an Integration Center job:

The source data is our MDF custom portlet and the target would be twice the time account object as you can see highlighted above (red area is deducting the amount from the annual leave bucket | green area is posting the new amount to the savings account - CET).

And here is where the magic happens – in the custom portlet we have the time account external codes hidden – so they are not visible in the UI but stored in the database:

We map these 2 fields in the different time account external codes:

The time account details are filled in as follows:

 

 

And that brings us to the end of this article. I hope this information was useful for your projects.

Before adopting the solution please evaluate the pros and cons with your customer.

Looking forward to your comments and seeing your use cases/experiences on this topic.

entry.title: Re: $batch process to delete single or multiple Vendor fr...
entry.content:
Hi @Rohan_Chauhan ,

Could you try Content-Type application/http for the changeset and application/json for the DELETE request:

--batch
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset_1

--changeset_1
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary

DELETE A_BusinessPartner(BusinessPartner='101127') HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

--changeset_1--
--batch--

and for the request itself use batch as the boundary:

Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=batch
Accept: application/json
X-CSRF-Token: <your_token>

You can read more on formatting batch request here.

Best,
Peter

entry.title: Maintain SAP-Events Header Fields.
entry.content:
SAP Business Accelerator Hub provides wide list of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Event Objects. By choosing a certain event object and its event references, you can see that like any other cloud events, each SAP event has a payload which carries detailed information of the event packet, known as a body, and a distinct header that describe what the event is. For example, looking at the event references of the creation of a sales order event we have:

From release 2023, attributes defined in the 'DATA' section of a business event or in other words, the body of the event, can be promoted to the header to become Custom Extension Context Attributes of the Cloud Event Context. As it’s known, the body contains the actual data being exchanged, and for security reason and unlike header data, it is not visible to the outside world. For example, imagine that based on a business requirement the would like to filter this type of event according to some event fields of the body. Hence, it is needed to make these fields visible by adding them to the header fields. Alternatively, the header attributes can also be used for source side filtering in the publisher system, either by the customer or an event middleware like Event Hub. Hereafter, I show you how easy you can expand header fields!

Add Data Fields to the Header of a Payload

 Before commencing, you should bear in mind that only those events developed in an extendable cloud object can be maintained. To explore the feasibility, in your system and under Released Objects, EXTENDED_IN_CLOUD_DEVELOPMENT; look for the behavior definition of the event you are interested in

In the relevant Behavior Definition find the related event parameter, in which the event is defined, for example in our case and for the event SalesOrderCreated, open the behavior definition ‘R_SALESORDERTP’ and check the related parameter for this event:

It is important that the abstract entity ‘D_SalesOrderCreated’ has the annotation showing that metadata extension is allowed, otherwise we are not able to extend that event.  

Now that our entity is extendable, create your own ABAP project, and by right clicking on your package, add new metadata extension for this entity as follows:

 

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
annotate entity D_SalesOrderCreated
  with 
{
@Event.context.attribute: 'xsapsotypesgb'
    SalesOrderType;
}

 

Beware that per event, i.e. parameter, only one meta data extension can be created. The main requirement is to add the annotation '@Event.context.attribute' for the respective field of a RAP entity which is used to define the business event type. There is also a naming convention for the attributes which are supposed to be promoted from the 'DATA' section of the event type into the Cloud Event Context as follows:

<extensionAttributeName>:=xsap<name>
  <name>:= name of the extension attribute

Please keep in mind that the XSAP prefix should be reserved for SAP delivered header attributes, but currently it is also mandatory for custom attributes. Besides, the type of system of the Cloud Event Context is more restrictive than the type of system of the event 'DATA' section. It means, only the following data types: Boolean, Integer, String, Binary, URI, and Timestamp are allowed. Hence, attributes which have been promoted from the 'DATA' section of the event type into the Cloud Event Context will be converted to the type 'String’ if their original data type is not allowed for the Cloud Event Context. More restriction can be found here.
Now, we save and activate the meta data extension, and change to the FLP to create a communication arrangement for the event enablement scenario (if we don’t have any) and maintain the channel as it is explained in my previous post. 
Then, we need to configure the outbound channel. After selecting your channel in ‘Enterprise Event Enablement’ application, click on ‘create’ and find the topic related to the selected SAP event. In our case, sales order created.

Now whenever a new sales order is created, an event will be raised, and you can see your modified header. For this, open the application ‘Enterprise Event Enablement-Event Monitor’, choose your channel, and open the related payload of the published event.

As you can see, the chosen attribute with the given name is shown in the header part. Now, you can use this attribute as a filter for the corresponding event type. In the Outbound Topics table, the column ‘Has Event Filters’ indicates whether filters are defined for the outbound topic. Go back to the Enterprise Event Enablement ‒ Configure Channel Binding app, choose your channel and select your outbound topic binding. To add a filter to the outbound topic, choose ‘Create Event Filter’. Under property, you can either choose your own defined property, in our case ‘xsapsotypessgb’, or use the standard context attributes.

The details of this process is nicely explained in this tutorial.

In the next blog post, I will explain to you how you can use an already implemented event with a different payload. So, stay put!

entry.title: Absence Quota Generation - PT60
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Can someone please suggest on the below requirement.

We have a requirement that whenever a user works overtime of 2 hours, the employee is eligible to take off for that 2 hours anytime in next 30 days.

For example, User X worked overtime of 8 hours on 01.11.2024, then post time evaluation, the user should get an absence quota balance with the validity date as 02.11.2024 + 30 days.

However, when the same user also worked overtime of 8 hours again on 02.11.2024, then another absence quota record to be created with 8 hours balance with the validity date as 03.11.2024+30 days, but should not summed up to the existing quota (validity 02.11.2024+30days).

I was able to generate only one absence quota with validity as record date + 30 days. However, we would like to have multiple absence quota records with validity dates based on the date of overtime worked.

Can someone please help me how to achieve this?

Many thanks in advance.

Regards,
Sai

entry.title: Re: Elimination Balance Sheet and P&L
entry.content:
• Group Reporting (GR) always stores periodic (PER) data regardless of how data was selected or loaded (YTD or PER). There are "conversion capabilities" at various places that support conversion of YTD <--> PER (e.g. file upload, analytics, etc.). 
  • When loading external data users can chose PER or YTD. A general recommendation is to load YTD to make sure YTD data is consistent between source and GR. System will calculate the delta for the processing period by deducting the already existing YTD of the processing period. This leads to a PER amount in GR stored in the processing period.
  • In analytics YTD is calculated by the system selecting periods 000-reporting period.
  • Eliminations can be configured with reclassification rules today. It can be configured how data is selected (YTD or PER). Hence BS can be configured YTD, PL can be configured PER. Same applies to currency translation.

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 01 - History of SAP Extended Warehouse Management
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 01 – The history of SAP EWM.

You can listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

A look back to the 90s!

Warehouse management is not a new topic at SAP. Already in 1991 SAP released LES-WM as part of SAP R/3. At the beginning of 2002 SAP decided to develop a new warehouse management product - SAP Extended Warehouse Management. There were several reasons for the new development for example increased performance requirements, a more flexible process design, the direct integration of material flow systems or mobile working capabilities.

In 2005 SAP published the first release of SAP EWM. And since 2005 SAP delivers continously new functions and innovations for EWM to its customers. That means SAP develops warehouse management solutions for more than 30 Years now. 

The technical foundation of SAP EWM has changed several times in the past. At the beginning it was implemented on the Supply Chain Management platform, later – with EWM 9.0 - it was also possible to run it as a standalone solution on the Netweaver stack. Usually always as a decentralized system.

Warehouse Management with SAP - More than 30 years of experience

 

The move to SAP S/4HANA

In 2015  we launched SAP S/4HANA – our new ERP flagship and the successor of SAP ECC 6.0

And since 2016 EWM is available on SAP S/4HANA. With SAP S/4HANA release 1610 and 1709 SAP EWM was only available as an integrated warehouse management solution - the so-called embedded EWM in S / 4HANA. Because it was very clear that a state-of-the-art warehouse management solution should be integral part of SAP S/4HANA. With the release 1809 in the year 2018, the decentralized deployment option for EWM on S/4HANA was launched too.

Today customers have the option to use EWM either within an S/4HANA system or still as a decentralized system - either connected to an SAP ECC or a SAP S/4HANA system via native SAP to SAP integration. The decision whether embedded or decentralized is the better choice for a customer depends on various aspects - functional, operational and strategic aspects. Our podcast episode 2 is about this decision. 

Options for inventory management and warehouse management with SAPBasic vs. advanced Warehousing

 

For embedded EWM in S/4HANA there are also two operating models: Basic and advanced warehousing. Basic warehousing describes the license-free option comparable with LE-WM in SAP ECC. It's from a commercial perspective part of SAP S/4HANA and offers already features such as multi-level processes, handling unit management or the complete mobile working environment.

Advanced warehousing allows customers to use the full range of EWM functions to run and optimize their warehouses. It offers for example an integrated material flow control, a highly flexible wave management, kitting and value added services, work in process handling and many more. Advanced warehousing is a "best of breed" warehouse management solution combined with SAP's "best of suite" offering. 

The operating model basic warehousing is only available with embedded EWM in S/4HANA. And customers can choose on warehouse number level if a warehouse runs with basic or advanced warehousing. A decentralized EWM on S/4HANA however is always "advanced warehousing". From a source code perspective, there is no difference between basic and advanced warehousing. The complete source code is always available.

High Level Overview Basic vs. advanced Warehousing

 

The future of warehouse management with SAP

SAP EWM  on S/4HANA is the successor of SAP LE-WM. The mainstream maintenance for LE-WM as part of SAP ECC will end at the latest by end of 2027. There are different options to move forward with existing LE-WM Implementations. 

As part of an SAP S/4HANA migration customers can continue to use LE-WM in SAP S/4HANA as so called compatibility scope until end of 2025 for example to migrate other modules first or not migrate all warehouses to EWM at once.

There is also option of Stock Room Management which is part of SAP S/4HANA and which can be used in S/4HANA also after 2025. From a source code perspective it is LE-WM however some functionalities such as the decentralized WM or the material flow connection (LE-WCU Interface) are no longer part of Stock Room Management. Stock Room Management enables customers to run LE-WM implementations for simple warehouses without any adjustments also in S/4HANA. There is a report which supports customers to check if existing LE-WM implementations are Stock Room Management compliant that means that the implementations do not use any features which are not covered by the usage rights of Stock Room Management. It also important to mention that no further developments are planned for Stock Room Management. 

Stock Room Management as a bridging technology on the way to SAP EWM

 

And therefore it makes sense to discuss the migration of all LE-WM implementations to EWM. For new warehouse implementations SAP EWM is recommended. With embedded EWM SAP offers an integrated warehouse management solution in SAP S/4HANA that meets the latest logistics requirements. It is seamlessly integrated in S/4HANA. A very good example are the synchronous postings of goods receipt for production directly onto SAP EWM storage bins with the well-know transaction MIGO. In addition, users benefit from the intuitive SAP FIORI strategy and warehouses can be managed based on real-time KPIs defined with capabilities of embedded analytics.

SAP EWM - A holistic and integrated intralogistics platform

 

SAP EWM offers customers the best of both worlds - a best of breed WMS which is natively integrated in to SAP's solution portfolio in a best of suite approach. 

Helpful Links: 

SAP S/4HANA Feature Scope Description (basic vs. advanced warehousing)
SAP Note 2881166 - FAQ Stock Room Management
SAP Note 2577428 - Roadmap for LE-WM in S/4HANA

Please visit our SAP EWM Podcast Landing Page with all Episodes, Blog Posts and Podcast Notes!

entry.title: Re: can I use CORRESPONDING # for deep internal tables?
entry.content:
I think you can use CORRESPONDING with MAPPING. Have you tried it?

Here is ABAP documentation link and example code from it:

DATA: BEGIN OF struct1,
        mcomp1 TYPE i VALUE 1,
        mcomp2 TYPE i VALUE 2,
        BEGIN OF substruc,
          subcomp1 TYPE i VALUE 1,
          subcomp2 TYPE i VALUE 2,
          subcomp3 TYPE i VALUE 3,
        END OF substruc,
      END OF struct1.

DATA: BEGIN OF struct2,
        comp2 TYPE i,
        comp1 TYPE i,
        BEGIN OF substruc,
          comp3 TYPE i,
          comp2 TYPE i,
          comp1 TYPE i,
        END OF substruc,
      END OF struct2.

struct2 =
  CORRESPONDING #(
    struct1 MAPPING comp1    = mcomp1
                    comp2    = mcomp2
                  ( substruc = substruc MAPPING comp1 = subcomp1
                                                comp2 = subcomp2
                                                comp3 = subcomp3 ) ).

5. Can Routing be configured in PLM?

6. If we use ECN in Teamcenter PLM then what can be the advantage of PEO? All BOM version changes can be done in PLM itself right? Why do we need to do some version changes in SAP PEO?

7. If new materials are created in Teamcenter PLM will they be created in SAP by integration automatically or need manual intervention?

entry.title: Don't Miss Out: Explore Key Improvements in SAP F&R with Collective Note 2335048
entry.content:
Dear SAP Forecasting and Replenishment  community,

Do you know already note 2335048 - F&R Improvements and Recommendations? If not, you are really missing out on important news! This note is a so called ‘collective note’ – it contains references to many other notes and all of them have one thing in common: they are no bug fixes but contain improvements, some smaller, some bigger! The note is updated on a regular basis with newly released relevant notes that include interesting new features.

Let’s have a look at some highlights of the recent improvements in more detail:

New Replenishment Types for Improving Multi-Echelon Replenishment Processes

There are 2 new replenishment types, 15 and 16. They behave very similarly like the ‘standard’ SAP F&R replenishment type 11 from the calculation logic but have some special twists:

  • RP type 15 can be used for products where a store order forecast is wanted for multi-echelon aggregation purposes, but no active order proposals should be generated for them with SAP F&R. So even if this might not be ideal (since the ‘real’ replenishment and store orders are done elsewhere and might differ quantity-wise), it is now possible to include these future demands for an aggregated store order forecast as a demand.
  • RP type 16 can be used to consider location products that are not ordered because of substitution locks in forecast and order forecast calculations. As a result, these numbers will also be considered by the aggregated order forecast calculation.

You can find more information in note 3415985 and its related notes.

Stock Projection can include Order Forecast

To understand the current and future stock of a location product the ‘Time Series Screen’ in SAP F&R is heavily used. It is part of the transaction ‘Cause Determination’ and is also embedded in the Replenishment Workbench and Product Workbench.  With note 3266842 you can now use as additional calculation basis options for the stock projection ‘Order Forecast’ and ‘Order Forecast with variable reorder point’ to include also potential requirements of future orders.

Additional Currencies When Working with the Replenishment Workbench

There is a so-called client currency in SAP F&R that is used when displaying and working with certain currency related values. In particular, when working with the Replenishment Workbench with different suppliers however, it might be useful to see some numbers in other currencies as well. With note 3117145 you can now define a user specific currency that is applied to the purchase and sales price fields in the navigation tree and the Order Proposal Item view as well. In addition to a user specific currency, you can specify a currency that is considered by the vendor restriction check. Check out the note for more details and how to set up variants that fit your needs. Note 3156854 helps you to work with selection variants that include additional currencies also in transaction /FRE/RWB_ORDNO (Single Order Proposal display).

Better Traceability with the Quick Overview

Sometimes it might be necessary to dig really into the details of an order proposal line item quantity. Even though there is already a lot of information on the line item view itself, this might not be sufficient to explain the result. This is where the so called ‘Quick Overview’ can help. Even though this comprehensive screen that is accessible out of the Replenishment or Product Workbench is available since 2020, it is important to know that it was continuously enhanced recently and covers now also ‘special‘ situations. If you do not know this useful screen please check it out -you will like it. No side-by-side excel calculations anymore – all important numbers are in one screen, divided by stock and demand period. Note  2862224 is the initial note that introduces the screen and there are multiple others for enhancements, e.g. 3422481 Quick Overview: Support of Requirements Calculation without a Stock Period 3489495 - Quick Overview - Optimization Quantities – TLO or Note 3214101 - Quick Overview: Safety Amount - Integration of Percentage Safety Amount for AOF.  All of these and more are also listed in the general collective note 2335048.

Order Forecast Improvements

Also not brand new but important to mention are these order forecast related notes: 2854849 - Order Forecast: Activation of time-dependent Unit of Measure and Reorder / Target Stock and 3292755 - MER: New option for determination of right border in AOF calculation.

  • By implementing enhancements /FRE/FU_FRP_BADI020 and /FRE/FU_FRP_BADI021), it is possible with the first note to consider time-dependent values for reorder point and target stock or different units of measure. As a result, more accuracy can be achieved.
  • Note 3292755 deals with the issue that a too short time horizon has been defined for some location product combinations, so that during the aggregation of the order forecast, this limits the entire data set. With this note you can now choose between an aligned horizon that considers this border and a maximum horizon that aggregates independently of what is available. This means that the result might not be consistent but since there were discussions to consider it nonetheless instead of nothing, this is now possible to choose!

This is just a glimpse of what has been done recently and also in the past years and I encourage you to browse through note 2335048 - F&R Improvements and Recommendations. I am sure you will find also other valuable points that help you improve your SAP Forecasting and Replenishment implementation.

Happy reading and kudos to our great support team!

entry.title: Where to view tickets that have been de-escalateded?
entry.content:
I have a question for your help.

Case：After Level 1 agent receives the customer's ticket, L1 agent finds that he can't solve it, so L1 agent escalate the ticket to L2. L2 agent can't solve it, so L2 agent escalate the ticket to L3. After L3 provides a solution, de-escalate the ticket to L2/ L1.

My problem: Tickets that have been de-escalateded by L3 people will no longer be able to appear in “My Tickets” page. If L3 wants to see tickets that have been de-escalateded to L1/L2, how can they find them？

Looking forward to your reply, thank you in advance！
